title: Idea: support for systemd’s mstack format
body:
Systemd v260 now supports a form of on-disk OCI storage format which it calls [mstack](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/devel/systemd.mstack.html). The release also comes with a utility to pull OCI images into these mstack directories ([importctl pull-oci](https://man.archlinux.org/man/importctl.1.en)).

It would be useful for skopeo to support this storage format. One use case is the creation of systemd-native services that run inside containers with the `RootMStack=/path/to/mstack` [option](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.exec.html#RootMStack=). Another is to use systemd-nspawn to run containers stored in different directories, since importctl only downloads the files into specific locations.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

So far, Skopeo (and the underlying c/image library) has stayed out of the business of _interpreting_ layer contents. Dealing with `tar` layers would already be fairly out of scope, and any generic support of the full “DDI” format, which seems to be “any kind of Linux-supported file systems” is just plain unreasonable in a cross-platform setting.

Skopeo’s `experimental-image-proxy` command can be used to integrate with Skopeo’s registry code (e.g. use the same registry configuration and credentials). But I think the more complex part would be _managing_ such a storage (reference counting, deduplication, handling concurrent uses / writes), and all of that code would be `mstack`-specific.

My idea would be for skopeo to just support mstack as a download target, in a manner similar to the `dir:` format. That would entail creating the layers as normal directories and generating the symlinks, but not worry about any of the DDI stuff. While mstack is a complex format to interpret, it’s not necessarily a complex format to generate.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
